payload: design, document, and build a voice that sequences screen events (sprites) rather than musical events. Use multiclaude to manage work

Sprite voice design complete. A new voice backend (lib/voices/sprite.lua) implements the standard voice interface but spawns visual events instead of audio. Parameter mapping: trigger=show/hide, note=shape(7 types), octave=Y position, duration=display time, velocity=size+opacity, ratchet=visual stutter, alt_note=X position/hue, glide=easing. Polymetric loop architecture creates complex evolving visual patterns. Same grid UI works unchanged. Seamstress screen API supports full RGBA, textures, geometry — sufficient for 60fps sprite rendering. 5-phase implementation plan in scratchpad. Key design questions: per-track voice type selection (MIDI vs sprite vs both), screen layout, shape palette expansion.

Approved with simplifications. Sprite voice is ADDITIVE — always fires alongside nb/MIDI, never replaces. Simplified SpriteEvent to 6 fields (shape, x, y, size, color, spawn_beat, duration). alt_note = X position only. No rotation, no easing, no sprite interaction. 7 shapes is enough. Full screen canvas, no UI chrome. Phases 1-4 only — no textures/trails/ratchet-stutter yet. nb support preserved on every track. Implementation order: sprite backend, renderer+shapes, sequencer wiring, tests. See scratchpad for full review.

Sprite voice phases 1-4 implemented. New: lib/voices/sprite.lua, lib/seamstress/sprite_render.lua, specs/sprite_spec.lua. Modified: sequencer.lua (play_sprite), app.lua (ctx.sprite_voices), re_kriate_seamstress.lua (wiring + full-screen render). 346 tests pass (318 existing + 28 new). Syntax clean.
